creator: dalibor.gudzic@gmail.com
text:
Upgrade from 7.1-RELEASE to 7.2-RELEASE prevents system from booting. It stops with message:

..
pcib5: <ACPI PCI-PCI bridge> at device 19.1 on pci0
pci5:  <ACPI PCI bus> on pcib5
ath0:  <Atheros 2413> mem 0xc9000000-0xc900ffff irq 18 at device 1.0 on pci5
ath0:  [ITHREAD]


Fatal trap 1: privileged instruction fault while in kernel mode
cpuid = 0; apic id = 00
instruction pointer             = 0x20:0x3
stack pointer                   = 0x28:0xc1020694
frame pointer                   = 0x28:0xc10206ac
code segment                    = base 0x0, limit 0xfffff, type 0x16
                                = DPL 0, pres 1, def32 1, gran 1
processor eflags                = interupt enabled, resume, IOPL = 0
current process                 = 0 (swapper)
trap number                     = 1
panic: privileged instruction fault
cpuid = 0
Uptime: 1s
Automatic reboot in 15 seconds - press any key on the console to abort



Here is the pciconf -lv|grep -A4 ath

ath0@pci0:5:1:0: class=0x020000 card=0x2052168c chip=0x001a168c rev=0x01 hdr=0x00
    vendor     = 'Atheros Communications Inc.'
    device     = 'Atheros AR5005G Atheros AR5005G 802.11abg NIC Chipset / TP-Link (TL-WN551G)'
    class      = network
    subclass   = ethernet


And devinfo -v|grep ath

            ath0 pnpinfo vendor=0x168c device=0x001a subvendor=0x168c subdevice=0x2052 class=0x020000 at slot=1 function=0


Full dmesg (with ath and ath_hal disabled):

I have submitted earlier a PR about the non-working ath (yeah, sorry for that garbage at the end):

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=kern/116444

Pls tell me if you need more output.

Fix: 

For the moment, disabling ath in GENERIC makes the system boot:

# grep ath /usr/src/sys/i386/conf/GENERIC

#device         ath             # Atheros pci/cardbus NIC's
#device         ath_hal         # Atheros HAL (Hardware Access Layer)
#device         ath_rate_sample # SampleRate tx rate control for ath
How-To-Repeat: Upgrade to 7.2-RELEASE and boot.
